---
title: "thresh_dhw"
author: "R. Cunning"
date: "2024-05-22"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(lme4)
library(tidyverse)
```

# Find the relationship between bleaching threshold (°C) and DHW accumulation

```{r}
# Import Florida Keys daily temperature data for 1985-2025
## Downloaded on 2026-02-16 from: https://coralreefwatch.noaa.gov/product/vs/data/florida_keys.txt
temp0 <- read_delim("data/temperature/florida_keys_temperature.txt", skip = 20, trim_ws = TRUE)
temp <- temp0 %>%
  mutate(temp = `SST@90th_HS`) %>%
  dplyr::select(YYYY, MM, DD, temp) %>%
  unite(YYYY, MM, DD, col = "date", sep = "-") %>%
  mutate(date = as_date(date),
         yday = yday(date),
         year = year(date))

# Define Maximum Monthly Mean temperature for Florida Keys
mmm <- 29.6264 # Actual MMM for FL Keys

# Plot temperature for each year, and MMM
mytemp <- temp %>% filter(year < 2025) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = yday, y = temp, color = factor(year), group = year, alpha = 0.5)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 29.624), lty = 2) +
  #geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 30.624), lty = 2) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "Day of year", y = "Temperature (°C)") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# DHWs accumulate when temperatures exceed the bleaching threshold, defined as MMM temperature + 1°C
# Simulate increased bleaching thresholds (i.e., MMM + 1°C + x), with x ranging from 0 to 1.5°C of additional increase
# Calculate DHWs for all simulated increased thresholds for each year of real temperature data
dhwout <- temp %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  nest() %>%
  crossing(add = seq(0, 2.5, 0.1)) %>%
  mutate(dhd = map2(data, add, ~if_else(.x$temp > mmm + 1 + .y, (.x$temp - mmm) / 7, 0)),
         dhw = map2(data, dhd, ~slider::slide_index_sum(.y, .x$yday, before = 84L)),
         maxdhw = map_dbl(dhw, max))

# Compute reduction in DHW in each year based on each increase in threshold
# Keep only years where at least 1 DHW was accumulated (based on actual MMM)
diffs <- dhwout %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  mutate(diff = maxdhw[add==0] - maxdhw) %>%
  filter(maxdhw > 0, max(maxdhw) > 2)

# Plot reduction in DHW for each year afforded by X°C increase in bleaching threshold
diffs_vs_add <- ggplot(diffs, aes(x = add, y = diff, group = year, color = factor(year))) + 
  #geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "Simulated increase in bleaching threshold (°C)",
       y = "Decrease in accumulated DHW")

# Model reduction in DHW as a function of simulated increase
## Constrain intercept to zero, and have random slopes for years
mod <- lmer(diff ~ 0 + add + (0 + add|year), data = diffs)
## Get slope and standard error
coefs <- tibble(
  dhw.per.degC = summary(mod)$coefficients[1],
  dhw.per.degC.se = summary(mod)$coefficients[2]
)

# Plot fitted model over simulated data
anno <- paste0("y = ", round(coefs[1], 2), " x")
(figs4 <- diffs_vs_add + 
    geom_abline(aes(slope = coefs$dhw.per.degC, intercept = 0),
                           alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 1.5) +
    scale_color_discrete(name = "Year") +
    annotate("text", label = anno, x = 1.5, y = 1, hjust = 1) +
    theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
          legend.key.size = unit(5, "mm")) +
   # guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 2)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1.7), ylim = c(0, 13), expand = FALSE))

ggsave(filename = "figures/FigS4.png", plot = figs4, width = 180, height = 120, units = "mm")
coefs

## Save slope and standard error for analysis in analysis_main
write_csv(coefs, "data/processed/dhw.per.degC.csv")
```





```{r download_sst_data}

# Pixel-level CoralTemp SST stacks for Florida Reef Tract (robust lon/lat indexing + scaling)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(purrr)
library(ncdf4)
library(terra)
library(stringr)

# ---- CONFIG ----
base_sst <- "https://www.star.nesdis.noaa.gov/pub/socd/mecb/crw/data/5km/v3.1_op/nc/v1.0/daily/sst"
years <- c(1997:2025)
start_mmdd <- "06-01"
end_mmdd   <- "12-31"
out_dir <- "data/coraltemp"

# Florida Reef Tract bbox (WGS84)
bbox <- list(lon_min = -83.525, lon_max = -78.975, lat_min = 23.975, lat_max = 28.025)

dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ---- BUILD DATE LIST (preserve Date class) ----
dates <- map(years, \(yy) seq.Date(as.Date(sprintf("%d-%s", yy, start_mmdd)),
                                   as.Date(sprintf("%d-%s", yy, end_mmdd)),
                                   by = "day")) |> 
  list_c()

# ---- URLS + DOWNLOAD (only missing files) ----
sst_urls  <- sprintf("%s/%d/coraltemp_v3.1_%s.nc", base_sst, year(dates), format(dates, "%Y%m%d"))
sst_files <- file.path(out_dir, basename(sst_urls))
miss <- which(!file.exists(sst_files))
#if (length(miss)) walk2(sst_urls[miss], sst_files[miss], \(u, f) download.file(u, f, mode = "wb"))

library(purrr)
library(furrr)
library(future)
# download in parallel
if (length(miss)) {
  plan(multisession, workers = 8) 
  future_walk2(
    sst_urls[miss], sst_files[miss],
    \(u, f) download.file(u, f, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
  )
}



# 
# 
# # ---- DERIVE CORRECT lon/lat INDICES FROM FILE COORDINATES (NO hard-coded indices) ----
# nc <- nc_open(sst_files[1]); on.exit(nc_close(nc), add = TRUE)
# lon <- ncvar_get(nc, "lon")
# lat <- ncvar_get(nc, "lat")
# 
# # some CoralTemp grids may store lat with negative sign; index using absolute lat if so
# lat_for_index <- if (median(lat, na.rm = TRUE) < 0) -lat else lat
# 
# lon_idx <- which(lon >= bbox$lon_min & lon <= bbox$lon_max)
# lat_idx <- which(lat_for_index >= bbox$lat_min & lat_for_index <= bbox$lat_max)
# 
# stopifnot(length(lon_idx) > 0, length(lat_idx) > 0)
# 
# lon_start <- min(lon_idx); nlon <- length(lon_idx)
# lat_start <- min(lat_idx); nlat <- length(lat_idx)
# 
# # ---- READ ONE DAY SST SUBSET AS SpatRaster (scale_factor applied once; units already °C) ----
# read_sst_subset <- function(ncfile, var = "analysed_sst",
#                             lon_start, nlon, lat_start, nlat,
#                             lon, lat, lat_for_index) {
# 
#   nc <- nc_open(ncfile)
#   on.exit(nc_close(nc), add = TRUE)
# 
#   v <- nc$var[[var]]
#   dim_names <- vapply(v$dim, `[[`, character(1), "name")
# 
#   # start/count in the variable's dim order
#   start <- rep(1L, length(dim_names))
#   count <- vapply(v$dim, `[[`, integer(1), "len")
# 
#   i_lon  <- match("lon",  dim_names)
#   i_lat  <- match("lat",  dim_names)
#   i_time <- match("time", dim_names)
# 
#   start[i_lon] <- lon_start; count[i_lon] <- nlon
#   start[i_lat] <- lat_start; count[i_lat] <- nlat
#   if (!is.na(i_time)) { start[i_time] <- 1L; count[i_time] <- 1L }
# 
#   # raw values (integers), so we can NA-out fill values BEFORE scaling
#   arr <- ncvar_get(nc, var, start = start, count = count, raw_datavals = TRUE) |> drop()
# 
#   fill <- ncatt_get(nc, var, "_FillValue")$value
#   if (is.null(fill) || is.na(fill)) fill <- ncatt_get(nc, var, "missing_value")$value
#   if (!is.null(fill) && !is.na(fill)) arr[arr == fill] <- NA
# 
#   sf <- ncatt_get(nc, var, "scale_factor")$value
#   if (!is.null(sf) && !is.na(sf)) arr <- arr * sf
# 
#   # extent from lon + (possibly sign-corrected) lat
#   lon_sub <- lon[lon_start:(lon_start + nlon - 1)]
#   lat_sub <- lat_for_index[lat_start:(lat_start + nlat - 1)]
# 
#   # [lon,lat] -> [lat,lon]
#   m <- t(arr)
# 
#   r <- rast(m)
#   ext(r) <- ext(min(lon_sub), max(lon_sub), min(lat_sub), max(lat_sub))
#   crs(r) <- "EPSG:4326"
# 
#   # keep north-up
#   if (length(lat_sub) > 1 && lat_sub[2] < lat_sub[1]) r <- flip(r, "vertical")
#   r
# }
# 
# # ---- BUILD SST STACK FOR A GIVEN YEAR ----
# sst_stack_by_year <- function(files, dates, yy, var = "analysed_sst",
#                               lon_start, nlon, lat_start, nlat,
#                               lon, lat, lat_for_index) {
# 
#   idx <- which(year(dates) == yy)
#   files_y <- files[idx]; dates_y <- dates[idx]
#   o <- order(dates_y)
#   files_y <- files_y[o]; dates_y <- dates_y[o]
# 
#   rlist <- map(files_y, ~read_sst_subset(.x, var, lon_start, nlon, lat_start, nlat, lon, lat, lat_for_index))
#   s <- rast(rlist)
#   time(s) <- dates_y
#   s
# }
# 
# # ---- EXAMPLE: BUILD 2014 STACK + CHECK VALUES ----
# sst2014 <- sst_stack_by_year(sst_files, dates, 2014,
#                             var = "analysed_sst",
#                             lon_start = lon_start, nlon = nlon,
#                             lat_start = lat_start, nlat = nlat,
#                             lon = lon, lat = lat, lat_for_index = lat_for_index)
# sst2015 <- sst_stack_by_year(sst_files, dates, 2015,
#                             var = "analysed_sst",
#                             lon_start = lon_start, nlon = nlon,
#                             lat_start = lat_start, nlat = nlat,
#                             lon = lon, lat = lat, lat_for_index = lat_for_index)
# sst2023 <- sst_stack_by_year(sst_files, dates, 2023,
#                             var = "analysed_sst",
#                             lon_start = lon_start, nlon = nlon,
#                             lat_start = lat_start, nlat = nlat,
#                             lon = lon, lat = lat, lat_for_index = lat_for_index)
# 
# 
# 
# # ---- 1) Load CRW 5-km climatology + compute MMM per pixel, subset to your Florida bbox ----
# clim_url  <- "https://www.star.nesdis.noaa.gov/pub/sod/mecb/crw/data/5km/v3.1_op/climatology/nc/ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc"
# clim_dir  <- "data/coraltemp/climatology"
# dir.create(clim_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
# clim_file <- file.path(clim_dir, "ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc")
# if (!file.exists(clim_file)) download.file(clim_url, clim_file, mode = "wb")
# 
# # We read monthly climatology SST (12 layers) and take pixelwise max = MMM
# clim <- rast(clim_file)
# 
# # Pick 12 monthly mean SST layers (this selector is robust across common naming patterns)
# sst_like <- grep("sst|temperature|temp", names(clim), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)
# stopifnot(length(sst_like) >= 12)
# clim12 <- clim[[sst_like[1:12]]]
# 
# mmm_all <- app(clim12, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
# names(mmm_all) <- "MMM"
# 
# library(terra)
# library(ncdf4)
# 
# # --- 1) MMM on the same grid as your SST stacks ---
# tmpl <- sst2014[[1]]
# clim <- rast(clim_file)
# 
# # Use MMM directly
# mmm <- clim[["sst_clim_mmm"]]
# 
# # align CRS and grid
# crs(mmm) <- crs(tmpl)
# mmm <- resample(mmm, tmpl, method = "bilinear")
# 
# summary(values(mmm))
# 
# # sanity check
# terra::global(mmm, fun = function(z) sum(!is.na(z)))
# 
# # --- 2) Functions: pixelwise maxDHW and slope(DHW decrease per °C threshold increase) ---
# maxdhw_for_add <- function(sst_stack, mmm_r, add, win = 84L) {
#   # keep only pixels where MMM exists (land pixels will be NA)
#   sst_stack <- mask(sst_stack, mmm_r)
# 
#   thr <- mmm_r + 1 + add
#   dhd <- ifel(sst_stack > thr, (sst_stack - mmm_r) / 7, 0)
# 
#   M <- as.matrix(dhd, wide = TRUE)  # pixels x days
#   nd <- ncol(M)
#   if (nd <= win) stop("Need >84 daily layers for DHW window; increase date range.")
# 
#   # pixels with at least one observed day
#   valid <- rowSums(!is.na(M)) > 0
# 
#   # treat missing days as 0 contribution (prevents NA poisoning in cumsum)
#   M[is.na(M)] <- 0
# 
#   cs <- t(apply(M, 1, cumsum))
#   rs <- cs
#   rs[, (win + 1):nd] <- cs[, (win + 1):nd] - cs[, 1:(nd - win)]
#   rs[, 1:win] <- NA_real_
# 
#   out <- apply(rs, 1, max, na.rm = TRUE)
#   out[!is.finite(out) | !valid] <- NA_real_
#   out
# }
# 
# pixel_slope_dhw_per_degC <- function(sst_stack, mmm_r, adds = seq(0, 2.0, 0.25), win = 84L) {
#   base <- maxdhw_for_add(sst_stack, mmm_r, add = 0, win = win)
# 
#   x <- adds[-1]
#   diffs <- vapply(
#     x,
#     \(a) base - maxdhw_for_add(sst_stack, mmm_r, add = a, win = win),
#     numeric(length(base))
#   )
# 
#   slope <- as.numeric(diffs %*% x) / sum(x^2)  # through-origin slope per pixel
# 
#   out <- mmm_r
#   values(out) <- slope
#   names(out) <- "DHW_per_degC"
#   out
# }
# 
# # --- 3) Run (2014 example) ---
# slope2014 <- pixel_slope_dhw_per_degC(sst2014, mmm, adds = seq(0, 2.0, 0.25), win = 84L)
# 
# summary(values(slope2014))
# 
# # Optional inverse map (°C per DHW)
# degC_per_DHW_2014 <- 1 / slope2014
# degC_per_DHW_2014
# 
# slope2015 <- pixel_slope_dhw_per_degC(sst2015, mmm)
# slope2023 <- pixel_slope_dhw_per_degC(sst2023, mmm)
# 
# summary(values(slope2015))
# summary(values(slope2023))

```


```{r}
# # Use regional MMM but run slope analysis across years within each pixel
# 
# mmm <- 29.6264
# adds <- seq(0, 2.5, 0.1)
# 
# # optional: faster/cleaner rolling maxdhw function
# max_dhw_from_temp <- function(temp, add, mmm, win = 84L) {
#   # temp: numeric vector (daily SST), may contain NA
#   if (all(is.na(temp))) return(NA_real_)
# 
#   # DHD rule (exactly your regional logic)
#   dhd <- ifelse(temp > (mmm + 1 + add), (temp - mmm) / 7, 0)
#   dhd[is.na(dhd)] <- NA_real_
# 
#   n <- length(dhd)
#   if (n < win) return(NA_real_)
# 
#   # rolling sum of last 'win' days using cumulative sums
#   cs <- c(0, cumsum(replace(dhd, is.na(dhd), 0)))
#   roll <- cs[(win + 1):(n + 1)] - cs[1:(n - win + 1)]
# 
#   # if there were NAs in dhd, the replace() trick treats them as 0.
#   # If you want to be strict (any NA contaminates a window), use the strict version below.
#   max(roll, na.rm = TRUE)
# }
# 
# # Values as matrices: ncell x nlyr
# m2014 <- values(sst2014, mat = TRUE)
# m2015 <- values(sst2015, mat = TRUE)
# m2023 <- values(sst2023, mat = TRUE)
# 
# stopifnot(nrow(m2014) == nrow(m2015), nrow(m2014) == nrow(m2023))
# n_cells <- nrow(m2014)
# 
# # per-pixel mixed model slope
# pixel_slope_one <- function(i) {
#   # collect diffs across years for this pixel
#   diffs_list <- list()
# 
#   for (yr in c("2014","2015","2023")) {
#     temp <- switch(
#       yr,
#       "2014" = m2014[i, ],
#       "2015" = m2015[i, ],
#       "2023" = m2023[i, ]
#     )
# 
#     if (all(is.na(temp))) next
# 
#     maxdhw <- vapply(adds, \(a) max_dhw_from_temp(temp, a, mmm), numeric(1))
# 
#     # replicate your year filter:
#     # keep only years where baseline maxdhw>0 and year reaches >2 somewhere
#     base <- maxdhw[adds == 0]
#     if (is.na(base) || base <= 0 || max(maxdhw, na.rm = TRUE) <= 2) next
# 
#     diffs_list[[yr]] <- tibble(
#       year = yr,
#       add  = adds,
#       diff = base - maxdhw
#     )
#   }
# 
#   diffs <- bind_rows(diffs_list)
# 
#   # need enough data to fit random slope for year
#   if (nrow(diffs) < 10 || dplyr::n_distinct(diffs$year) < 2) return(NA_real_)
# 
#   # your model: intercept forced to zero, random slopes for year
#   mod <- try(lmer(diff ~ 0 + add + (0 + add | year), data = diffs), silent = TRUE)
#   if (inherits(mod, "try-error")) return(NA_real_)
# 
#   unname(fixef(mod)[["add"]])
# }
# 
# # Run across all pixels and write to a slope raster
# # run (base R loop; you can swap in pbapply/parallel later if needed)
# slopes <- vapply(seq_len(n_cells), pixel_slope_one, numeric(1))
# 
# slope_pix <- sst2014[[1]]         # template
# values(slope_pix) <- slopes
# names(slope_pix) <- "DHW_per_degC_mixed"
# 
# summary(values(slope_pix))
# plot(slope_pix)
# 
# plot(sst2014)

```

# unified workflow-- i think this works, just need to download more years of data
```{r}
############################################################
## PIXEL-SPECIFIC ΔDHW vs Δ°C SLOPES (MIXED MODEL, EXACTLY
## FOLLOWING YOUR REGIONAL PIPELINE) — REEF PIXELS ONLY
############################################################

library(terra)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(slider)
library(lme4)

# ----------------------------
# User inputs / assumptions
# ----------------------------
# - You have NetCDF SST files for MANY years in a folder, one file per day (or per time),
#   OR otherwise a set of NetCDFs that terra::rast() can read into a time-aware SpatRaster.
# - my_hull is an sf/SpatVector polygon defining reef extent; we'll mask to it.
# - The SST variable name/subdataset varies by product; set `sst_subds` correctly.
#   If you already know it (e.g., "sea_surface_temperature" or similar), set it here.

sst_dir   <- "data/coraltemp"            # <-- change if needed
sst_subds <- "analysed_sst"              # <-- set to e.g. "sea_surface_temperature" if your NetCDF needs it
mmm <- 29.6264                     # fixed regional MMM used for ALL pixels
adds <- seq(0, 2.5, 0.1)           # simulated threshold increases (°C)
win_days <- 84L                    # DHW window (84 days)

# ----------------------------
# 1) Load ALL SST, keep Jul–Dec, crop/mask to reef hull, fix flip
# ----------------------------
nc_files <- list.files(sst_dir, pattern = "\\.nc$", full.names = TRUE)

stopifnot(length(nc_files) > 0)

# Read as a multi-layer SpatRaster; if your NetCDF requires a variable/subdataset name, set sst_subds above.
sst_all <- if (is.null(sst_subds)) {
  rast(nc_files)
} else {
  rast(nc_files, subds = sst_subds)
}

# Ensure raster has a time vector; if not, you must supply dates from filenames.
# Many CRW products carry time in the file; if yours doesn't, you can parse from filenames here.
tt <- time(sst_all)
if (is.null(tt) || all(is.na(tt))) {
  # Fallback: try to parse YYYYMMDD from filenames (edit pattern to match your files)
  dates <- as.Date(stringr::str_extract(basename(nc_files), "\\d{8}"), format = "%Y%m%d")
  stopifnot(!all(is.na(dates)))
  time(sst_all) <- dates
  tt <- time(sst_all)
}

# Keep only July–December across all years
keep <- month(tt) %in% 7:12
sst_all <- sst_all[[which(keep)]]

# Crop & mask to reef hull (my_hull can be sf; terra can convert)
sst_all <- crop(sst_all, vect(my_hull))
sst_all <- mask(sst_all, vect(my_hull))

# Fix the vertical flip you discovered (do this BEFORE extracting values)
sst_all <- flip(sst_all, direction = "vertical")

# Reef cells = non-NA after mask in any representative layer (use first layer)
reef_cells <- which(!is.na(values(sst_all[[1]])))
stopifnot(length(reef_cells) > 0)

# ----------------------------
# 2) Build long tibble: pixel, year, date, yday, temp
#    (reef pixels only)
# ----------------------------
# dates <- as.Date(time(sst_all))
# nT <- nlyr(sst_all)
# stopifnot(length(dates) == ncol(sst_mat))

# Extract values for reef cells into a matrix: rows=cells, cols=time
# values(..., mat=TRUE) returns all cells; we subset to reef_cells
sst_mat <- values(sst_all, mat = TRUE)[reef_cells, , drop = FALSE]
sst_mat <- as.matrix(sst_mat)

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

# 1) Force unique, predictable column names for the matrix
sst_df <- as.data.frame(sst_mat, check.names = FALSE)
names(sst_df) <- paste0("t", seq_len(ncol(sst_df)))  # t1, t2, ..., tN

# 2) Now you can safely build temp_long
dates <- as.Date(time(sst_all))
stopifnot(length(dates) == ncol(sst_df))

temp_long <- as_tibble(sst_df) %>%
  mutate(pixel = reef_cells) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("t"),
    names_to = "tname",
    values_to = "temp"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    time_id = as.integer(sub("^t", "", tname)),
    date = dates[time_id],
    yday = yday(date),
    year = year(date)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(pixel, year, date, yday, temp) %>%
  arrange(pixel, date)
temp_long

# ----------------------------
# 3) EXACT same DHW simulation workflow per pixel,
#    then mixed model diff ~ 0 + add + (0 + add | year)
# ----------------------------

# Helper: compute max DHW for one year of daily temps at a given add
# (this mirrors your logic: dhd = if temp > mmm+1+add then (temp-mmm)/7 else 0,
#  dhw = 84-day rolling sum over yday/date, maxdhw = max(dhw))
maxdhw_one <- function(df_year, add, mmm, win_days) {
  dhd <- if_else(df_year$temp > (mmm + 1 + add), (df_year$temp - mmm) / 7, 0)
  dhw <- slider::slide_index_sum(dhd, df_year$date, before = win_days)
  max(dhw, na.rm = TRUE)
}

#df_pix <- temp_long %>% filter(pixel == 7)
# Per-pixel pipeline: returns slope + SE from the mixed model
fit_pixel <- function(df_pix) {
  # simulate maxdhw for each year × add
  dhwout <- df_pix %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    nest() %>%
    crossing(add = adds) %>%
    mutate(
      maxdhw = map2_dbl(data, add, ~maxdhw_one(.x, .y, mmm = mmm, win_days = win_days))
    )

  # compute reduction in DHW relative to add==0 baseline within each year
  diffs <- dhwout %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    mutate(diff = maxdhw[add == 0] - maxdhw) %>%
    # same filters you used regionally
    filter(maxdhw > 0, max(maxdhw, na.rm = TRUE) > 2) %>%
    ungroup()

  # need >=2 years to fit (0+add|year)
  if (n_distinct(diffs$year) < 2) {
    return(tibble(slope = NA_real_, se = NA_real_))
  }

  mod <- lmer(diff ~ 0 + add + (0 + add | year), data = diffs)

  # extract fixed slope + SE exactly as you did
  s <- summary(mod)$coefficients["add", "Estimate"]
  se <- summary(mod)$coefficients["add", "Std. Error"]

  tibble(slope = as.numeric(s), se = as.numeric(se))
}

# Run per pixel with nesting (tidyverse style)
pixel_slopes2 <- temp_long %>%
  group_by(pixel) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    fit = map(data, ~tryCatch(fit_pixel(.x), error = function(e) tibble(slope = NA_real_, se = NA_real_)))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(pixel, fit) %>%
  unnest(fit) %>%
  ungroup()

pixel_slopes2
# -> tibble: pixel, slope (DHW per °C), se

# ----------------------------
# 4) (Optional) Write slopes back to a raster for mapping / extraction later
# ----------------------------
slope_r <- sst_all[[1]]
values(slope_r) <- NA_real_
values(slope_r)[pixel_slopes2$pixel] <- pixel_slopes2$slope
names(slope_r) <- "DHW_per_degC_mixed"

# quick check
summary(values(slope_r))
plot(flip(slope_r))


### great this worked. now need to confirm how to make sure pixel id in the slopes data matches pixel id in the drm data
```





# Species' ∆ED50s pixel-abundance-weighted
```{r}
d.ed50 <- sp.ed50conf.24 %>%
  filter(year == "2023-2014") %>%
  rename(d.ed50 = ed50)
d.ed50


# Extract pixel slopes for 2014
pixel_slopes <- tibble(
  pixel = 1:ncell(slope2014),
  slope = values(slope2014)
)
# Remove NA pixels
pixel_slopes <- pixel_slopes %>%
  filter(!is.na(slope))

# Species abundance per pixel
sp_pixel <- drm_data_f %>%
  group_by(pixel, Species) %>%
  summarise(n = dplyr::n(), .groups="drop")

# Compute species-weighted slopes of dDHW vs ddegC
sp_slopes <- sp_pixel %>%
  left_join(pixel_slopes, by="pixel") %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    slope_sp = sum(n * slope, na.rm=TRUE) / sum(n),
    .groups="drop"
  )

# Merge with dED50 table
summary(slope2014)
d.ed50_new <- d.ed50 %>%
  left_join(sp_slopes, by="Species") %>%
  mutate(degC.sp = d.ed50 / slope_sp,
         degC = d.ed50 / 5.706)

d.ed50_new
ggplot(d.ed50_new, aes(x = Species, y = degC.sp - degC)) +
  geom_col()
```
